## Summary

When AVD is enabled and FSLogix profile storage is set to **SOFS (Scale-Out File Server)**, the Volume Detail page still generates `AVD-Profiles` and `AVD-OfficeContainers` as standalone Azure Local volume suggestions. This is wrong ‚Äî those profiles live **inside the SOFS guest cluster**, not on dedicated Azure Local CSVs. The capacity is already accounted for by the `SOFS-ProfileData` volume that SOFS generates. The result is that users are told to create volumes they don't need, and are shown double-counted storage requirements.

## Root Cause

In `src/engine/workload-volumes.ts`, the AVD section unconditionally generates `AVD-Profiles` and `AVD-OfficeContainers` whenever AVD is enabled and the relevant sizes are non-zero:

```typescript
if (avd.profileStorageWithGrowthTB > 0) {
  suggestions.push({ name: 'AVD-Profiles', ... })   // ‚Üê no check for profileStorageLocation
}
if (avd.totalOfficeContainerStorageTB > 0) {
  suggestions.push({ name: 'AVD-OfficeContainers', ... })   // ‚Üê no check
}
```

There is zero check for whether `profileStorageLocation === 'sofs'`. The `avdInputs` / per-pool `profileStorageLocation` is not passed into `generateWorkloadVolumes()` at all.

## Correct Behaviour by Storage Location

| AVD pool `profileStorageLocation` | Profiles volume on Azure Local? | Office containers on Azure Local? |
|---|---|---|
| `'sofs'` | ‚ùå No ‚Äî lives on SOFS CSV, already in `SOFS-ProfileData` | ‚ùå No ‚Äî same |
| `'s3d'` / `'azurefiles'` / other | ‚úÖ Yes ‚Äî needs its own Azure Local volume | ‚úÖ Yes |

## Expected Behaviour

When **all** AVD pools targeting FSLogix use `profileStorageLocation: 'sofs'`:
- `AVD-Profiles` volume suggestion is **suppressed**
- `AVD-OfficeContainers` volume suggestion is **suppressed**
- `SOFS-ProfileData` (from the SOFS section) already covers this storage ‚Äî no double-counting

When **any** AVD pool uses a non-SOFS profile location:
- Generate `AVD-Profiles` and `AVD-OfficeContainers` as today (scoped to non-SOFS pools only)

Mixed case (some pools SOFS, some not): generate volumes sized only for the non-SOFS pool users.

## Example (Current ‚Äî Wrong)

AVD enabled, 1 pool, `profileStorageLocation: 'sofs'`, SOFS enabled:

```
AVD section suggests:
  AVD-HostPool1-OS       ‚úÖ correct
  AVD-Profiles           ‚ùå WRONG ‚Äî this is on the SOFS CSV, not a separate Azure Local volume
  AVD-OfficeContainers   ‚ùå WRONG ‚Äî same

SOFS section suggests:
  SOFS-ProfileData       ‚úÖ correct ‚Äî this IS the profile storage
  SOFS-OsDisk            ‚úÖ correct
```

## Example (Expected ‚Äî Fixed)

```
AVD section suggests:
  AVD-HostPool1-OS       ‚úÖ
  (AVD-Profiles suppressed ‚Äî storage is on SOFS)
  (AVD-OfficeContainers suppressed ‚Äî storage is on SOFS)

SOFS section suggests:
  SOFS-ProfileData       ‚úÖ
  SOFS-OsDisk            ‚úÖ
```

## Implementation Notes

- `generateWorkloadVolumes()` needs access to per-pool `profileStorageLocation` ‚Äî either pass `avdInputs` in, or add a derived flag to `AvdResult` (e.g. `allPoolsUseSofs: boolean`, `nonSofsProfileStorageTB: number`)
- Simplest approach: add `avdInputs` to `WorkloadVolumeInputs` and check `avdInputs.pools.every(p => p.profileStorageLocation === 'sofs')` before generating the profile/office volumes
- For the mixed case: filter pools where `profileStorageLocation !== 'sofs'` and sum their `profileStorageWithGrowthTB` proportionally
- Files: `src/engine/workload-volumes.ts`, `src/pages/VolumesPage.tsx` (to pass `avdInputs`)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AVD-Profiles` not generated when all pools use `profileStorageLocation: 'sofs'`
- [ ] `AVD-OfficeContainers` not generated when all pools use `profileStorageLocation: 'sofs'`
- [ ] Both volumes still generated when pools use non-SOFS profile storage
- [ ] Mixed case: volumes sized for non-SOFS pools only
- [ ] `SOFS-ProfileData` still generated by SOFS section (no change there)
- [ ] Unit tests for all three cases (all-SOFS, all-non-SOFS, mixed)
